Title: Cationic amino acid transporters (CAT) enhance accumulation and susceptibility to the systemic herbicide L-phosphinothricin

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cationic amino acid transporter (CAT), SLC7 family transporter with multiple transmembrane helices
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Broad-spectrum systemic herbicide; glutamine synthetase inhibitor
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Plant tissues/whole thallus or seedling tissue
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes PPT uptake/accumulation and increases PPT susceptibility
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtCAT1 was strongly induced by PPT and MetSox in Arabidopsis transcriptome analysis and selected as a PPT/glutamine transporter candidate. 2. Overexpression of AtCAT1 in Marchantia polymorpha conferred strong PPT sensitivity and increased PPT accumulation. 3. AtCAT1 overexpression enabled growth on glutamine and glutamate as sole nitrogen sources, supporting a role in endogenous amino acid/nitrogen transport. 4. Arabidopsis cat mutants showed reduced PPT uptake and partial resistance, indicating AtCAT1 contributes to PPT accumulation in planta.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomics; heterologous overexpression in Marchantia polymorpha; herbicide sensitivity assays; LC-MS/MS metabolite uptake quantification; Arabidopsis knockout analysis; nitrogen-source growth assays
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed in Marchantia polymorpha overexpression lines; endogenous function assessed in Arabidopsis
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: MpEF1α promoter (constitutive) in Marchantia overexpression lines
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: PPT treatment; MetSox treatment; glutamine supplementation; nitrogen source substitution
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss-of-function contributes to dysregulation of stress and metabolic processes when combined with AtCAT5/AtCAT8 mutations; transporter redundancy limits full resistance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because it is a transporter rather than enzyme or TF. Required for glutamic acid uptake and endogenous nitrogen metabolism in Arabidopsis according to the paper.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Knockout or attenuation could contribute to partial PPT resistance, but likely requires stacking with other transporter edits because of redundancy and possible nitrogen-metabolism costs.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cationic amino acid transporter (CAT), SLC7 family transporter with 12-14 transmembrane helices
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Broad-spectrum systemic herbicide; glutamine synthetase inhibitor
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Plant tissues/whole thallus or seedling tissue
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes PPT uptake/accumulation and confers highest PPT susceptibility among tested transporters
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtCAT5 was strongly induced by PPT and MetSox and downregulated by glutamine, consistent with a shared PPT/amino-acid transport role. 2. Overexpression of AtCAT5 in Marchantia polymorpha caused the strongest PPT hypersensitivity among tested transporters and markedly increased tissue PPT accumulation. 3. Time-course analysis showed progressive PPT accumulation in AtCAT5-overexpressing lines. 4. Arabidopsis cat5 and cat1,5,8 mutants showed partial PPT resistance and reduced PPT uptake, supporting endogenous contribution of AtCAT5 to susceptibility. 5. Molecular dynamics identified key PPT-binding residues and hinge glycines controlling transport-associated conformational change.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomics; heterologous overexpression in Marchantia polymorpha; herbicide sensitivity assays; LC-MS/MS uptake quantification; Arabidopsis knockout analysis; molecular dynamics simulation; site-directed mutagenesis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed in Marchantia polymorpha overexpression lines; endogenous function assessed in Arabidopsis
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: MpEF1α promoter (constitutive) in Marchantia overexpression lines
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: PPT treatment; MetSox treatment; glutamine supplementation; nitrogen source substitution
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutational disruption of transport function may compromise essential amino acid transport and nitrogen metabolism; transporter redundancy limits complete resistance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Key residues involved in PPT binding include Val81, Gly83, Ala84, Gly85, Phe269, Ala270, Gly272, and Gly273. Gly272/Gly273 are critical for helix flexibility and intracellular tunnel opening.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major candidate for engineering PPT resistance via loss-of-function or substrate-discrimination editing, but edits may impair amino acid transport; may need multiplex editing with AtCAT1/AtCAT8-related transporters.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cationic amino acid transporter (CAT), SLC7 family transporter
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Broad-spectrum systemic herbicide; glutamine synthetase inhibitor
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes redundantly to PPT accumulation and susceptibility; loss with AtCAT1/AtCAT5 increases partial resistance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtCAT8 forms a phylogenetic subbranch with AtCAT1 and AtCAT5 and has overlapping substrate range/localization. 2. Triple knockout cat1,5,8 showed reduced PPT uptake and partial resistance. 3. Transcriptome analysis of cat mutants indicated collective AtCAT1/5/8 function is linked to amino acid and energy metabolism, but redundancy masks single-gene effects.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; Arabidopsis knockout mutant phenotyping; LC-MS/MS uptake quantification; basal transcriptomics
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Combined knockout perturbs core metabolic and stress-response pathways, implying fitness or metabolic costs
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core gene because the Results section directly tests the cat1,5,8 branch for PPT resistance, although AtCAT8 was not overexpressed individually.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as part of multiplex transporter editing strategies to achieve partial PPT resistance, but likely with metabolic tradeoffs and incomplete resistance due to redundancy.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Broad-spectrum systemic herbicide; glutamine synthetase inhibitor
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a low-efficiency PPT transporter causing only mild PPT sensitivity when overexpressed
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtAAP4 was strongly induced by PPT and MetSox and selected as a candidate transporter. 2. Overexpression in Marchantia caused only mild PPT sensitivity in higher-expressing lines. 3. PPT was not detected in AtAAP4ox-2 under the assay conditions, indicating low-efficiency transport relative to AtCAT1 and AtCAT5.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomics; heterologous overexpression in Marchantia polymorpha; herbicide sensitivity assays; LC-MS/MS uptake quantification
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed in Marchantia polymorpha overexpression lines
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: MpEF1α promoter (constitutive) in Marchantia overexpression lines
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: PPT treatment; MetSox treatment; glutamine supplementation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core gene because directly functionally tested in Results, but evidence supports only low-efficiency PPT transport.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lower-priority engineering target than CAT transporters, but may contribute to residual uptake and could be considered in transporter-stacking strategies.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Broad-spectrum systemic herbicide; glutamine synthetase inhibitor
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No detectable contribution to PPT susceptibility in the heterologous Marchantia assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtAAP6 was transcriptionally induced by PPT and MetSox and downregulated by glutamine in Arabidopsis. 2. Overexpression in Marchantia did not cause deleterious phenotypes upon 100 µM PPT treatment, arguing against an efficient PPT transport role under tested conditions.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomics; heterologous overexpression in Marchantia polymorpha; herbicide sensitivity assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed in Marchantia polymorpha overexpression lines
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: MpEF1α promoter (constitutive) in Marchantia overexpression lines
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: PPT treatment; MetSox treatment; glutamine supplementation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally tested candidate but negative/weak evidence for PPT transport in this study.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited value based on current evidence; not a priority target for PPT-resistance engineering.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Mutated cationic amino acid transporter (CAT), SLC7 family transporter
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Broad-spectrum systemic herbicide; glutamine synthetase inhibitor
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation abolishes transport-associated susceptibility, conferring PPT resistance comparable to Tak-1 despite modeled ligand affinity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Molecular dynamics predicted G272 and G273 are hinge residues required for H6B conformational change and tunnel opening during PPT passage. 2. The G272W/G273W mutant retained comparable modeled PPT affinity but lacked directional helix movement. 3. Overexpression of AtCAT5-G272W/G273W in Marchantia failed to confer PPT sensitivity, validating the importance of transport dynamics rather than binding alone.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Molecular dynamics simulation; site-directed mutagenesis; heterologous overexpression in Marchantia polymorpha; herbicide sensitivity assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed in Marchantia polymorpha overexpression lines
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: MpEF1α promoter (constitutive) in Marchantia overexpression lines
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely compromises normal amino acid transport function
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Engineered mutant construct rather than native gene; included because it is directly tested as a mechanistic derivative of a core transporter.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Illustrates a possible substrate-transport uncoupling strategy for resistance engineering, though likely constrained by amino acid transport defects.
